team_members.name: Robinson Burkey
team_members.notes: Former Head of Crypto at Jump Trading. Wormhole was originally incubated inside Jump Crypto before spinning out as an independent foundation in 2023.

team_members.name: Dan Reecer
team_members.role: Co-Founder & Chief Growth Officer, Wormhole Foundation
team_members.notes: Previously VP of Growth at Acala. Active on X/Twitter (@danreecer_). Key public face for ecosystem growth.

team_members.name: Hendrik Hofstadt
team_members.notes: Listed as core contributor in Wormhole GitHub and blog posts; LinkedIn profile not prominently surfaced in free search.

team_members.name: Attilio Silvio Pacifici
team_members.notes: Referenced in foundation governance documents; detailed LinkedIn not freely indexed.

investors.notes: Original incubator and largest early backer. Received W token allocation; vesting schedule per tokenomics doc shows 4-year linear vest with 1-year cliff for team/investors. Jump also covered the $320M 2022 hack reimbursement, cementing deep financial ties.
Participated in the 2023 private fundraise round. Confirmed via Wormhole blog announcement (wormhole.com/blog) and Multicoin public portfolio page.
Listed as strategic investor in Wormhole Foundation's 2023 fundraise announcement.
Participated in the $225M private fundraise announced August 2023 at a reported $2.5B valuation. Source: Wormhole blog + press coverage.
Named in the August 2023 $225M raise announcement.
Named in the August 2023 $225M raise announcement.

investors.investor_name: Public Token Sale / Airdrop (W token launch)
investors.notes: W token launched April 3, 2024 on Solana and EVM chains. Community/airdrop allocation ~17% of 10B total supply. Circulating supply at launch ~18% of total. Source: wormhole.com/token

legal_events.description: Wormhole bridge exploited for ~120,000 wETH (~$320M USD) via a signature verification vulnerability. Jump Crypto replenished funds within 24 hours. No criminal charges filed publicly; no SEC action recorded.

Wormhole Foundation formally incorporated as a Cayman Islands foundation company, separating from Jump Crypto. This is a standard offshore legal structure for crypto protocols; no adverse regulatory action associated.

SEC EDGAR free-text search returns NO filings under 'Wormhole Foundation' or 'W token'. No Reg D, Form D, or enforcement action found. Token launch proceeded without registered securities offering disclosure. NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER for any confidential SEC correspondence.

Jump Crypto (parent of original Wormhole team) named in broader regulatory scrutiny of crypto market makers following FTX collapse; no direct Wormhole-specific enforcement action. CFTC investigation of Jump Trading reported by Bloomberg; Wormhole Foundation stated it operates independently.

founder_credibility_summary: The founding team is fully doxxed with verifiable professional histories at Jump Trading and Acala, lending institutional credibility; however, the project's deep roots inside Jump Crypto—a firm under CFTC scrutiny—and the $320M 2022 bridge hack represent material reputational risks that the team has partially offset by spinning out into an independent foundation and securing a $225M raise at a $2.5B valuation.
vc_overhang_summary: Approximately 82% of the 10 billion W token supply was locked at launch (team, investors, foundation reserves, ecosystem fund), with a reported 1-year cliff and 4-year linear vest for insiders, meaning significant VC and Jump Crypto supply begins unlocking in Q2 2025 and continues through 2028, creating sustained sell-pressure risk. Exact per-investor ownership percentages are NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER as no on-chain vesting contract addresses have been publicly mapped to named investors.
alignment_summary: Insider and retail incentives are partially misaligned: retail holders received ~17% of supply via airdrop/community allocation at launch while insiders (team + investors + Jump) control the majority of supply under multi-year vests. The foundation's stated mission (open interoperability) aligns directionally with token appreciation, but the large VC overhang, Jump's conflict-of-interest as both incubator and guardian node operator, and the absence of on-chain governance rights for W holders at launch all tilt structural power toward insiders. Alignment improves if governance is progressively decentralized as roadmapped.
